title: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs)
concept: A Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) represents an entire 3D scene as a trained neural network that can render photorealistic views from any new camera angle, learned purely from a set of 2D photos of that scene.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why does forcing a point cloud into an arbitrary 2D grid shape hurt a standard CNN's performance on it?

options.text: There's no meaningful spatial order for scattered 3D points, so the arbitrary grid ordering introduces noise
Standard CNNs always perform best on any point cloud task
Point clouds cannot be represented in any digital format

exercises.explain: Yes! Forcing an arbitrary grid order ignores the point cloud's true unordered structure.

cards.title: A scene stored INSIDE a neural network
cards.body: What if, instead of storing a scene as pixels, points, or meshes, you could store the ENTIRE scene inside the weights of a trained neural network — and simply ask that network 'what does this scene look like from this new camera angle?'

cards.title: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF)
cards.body: A NeRF is a neural network trained on a set of 2D photos of a scene taken from different angles. Once trained, the network can generate a photorealistic image of that scene from ANY new camera viewpoint — including angles never actually photographed — by learning the scene's color and density at every 3D point.

cards.title: A movie set you can walk through virtually
cards.body: A NeRF is like turning a handful of still photographs of a movie set into a fully explorable, walk-through virtual set — you can move the camera to any position, even ones the original photographer never stood at, and the network renders a convincing new photo from that new spot.

cards.title: Reconstructing a historical monument
cards.body: Take 50 photos of a statue from different angles around it. Train a NeRF on those 50 photos. Afterward, you can generate a smooth video flying around the statue from ANY angle — including viewpoints that were never in your original 50 photos, purely from what the network learned.

cards.title: NeRFs aren't stored as explicit 3D geometry
cards.body: It's a myth that a NeRF works like a point cloud or 3D mesh you could directly export and edit in 3D software. A NeRF is fundamentally a trained NETWORK function — the '3D scene' only exists implicitly inside its learned weights, and extracting explicit geometry from it requires an additional conversion step.

cards.title: Explicit 3D Representations vs. NeRF
cards.body: Two very different ways to store a 3D scene:

left.text:
Explicit geometry (points, triangles)
Directly editable in 3D software
Fixed representation, not learned

right.text:
Implicit representation inside network weights
Renders any new photorealistic viewpoint
Learned from a set of 2D photos

exercises.prompt: What does a trained Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) allow you to do?

options.text: Render a photorealistic image of a scene from any new camera viewpoint, including ones never photographed
Compress a 2D image into a smaller file size
Classify an image into a fixed set of categories
Detect and label objects with bounding boxes

exercises.explain: Correct! NeRFs let you generate new photorealistic viewpoints of a learned scene.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A NeRF stores a scene as explicit, directly editable 3D geometry, just like a point cloud or mesh.
exercises.explain: False. A NeRF stores the scene implicitly inside its trained network weights, not as explicit editable geometry.

exercises.sentence: A NeRF is trained on a set of 2D ___ taken from different camera angles around a scene.
exercises.explain: NeRFs learn purely from a collection of 2D photographs of the scene.

exercises.prompt: Why is turning 50 photos of a statue into a smoothly explorable video from ANY angle a good example of NeRF's key capability?

options.text: It demonstrates rendering photorealistic views from camera angles that were never actually photographed, purely from learned network weights
It shows NeRF can only reproduce the exact original 50 photos and nothing else
It demonstrates NeRF requires no training photos at all

exercises.explain: Correct! Generating novel, unphotographed viewpoints is exactly what makes NeRF powerful.

exercises.prompt: A film studio wants to create a smooth virtual camera fly-through of a real historical location using only a modest set of photos taken from a few fixed spots. Which technique is purpose-built for this?

options.text: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF), which learn to render photorealistic novel viewpoints from a set of 2D photos
Standard image classification, which only assigns category labels
Basic 2D data augmentation like horizontal flipping
None of these techniques can generate new camera viewpoints

exercises.explain: Exactly! NeRF is specifically designed for exactly this kind of novel-viewpoint rendering task. Next: tying together 3D vision's real-world applications.
